title: The Oracle’s Shadow
subtitle: Urban conspiracy, factions, secrets and consequences
description: Urban investigation campaign for testing narrative memory, factions, revealable secrets, pressure clocks and session prep in Valdris.
summary: The adventurers arrive in Valdris following a prophecy that turns out to be a magical forgery. To expose the Oracle, they must connect clues, negotiate with incompatible factions and decide how to reveal a truth that could break the city.
recommendedFor: DMs who want to run a dense investigation campaign without preparing from scratch: a living city, factions with incompatible interests, clues connected to secrets, pressure clocks and table-ready sessions.
pitch: A fantasy intrigue campaign centered on Valdris, a port city ruled by fear of the Oracle. The premade shows how DMCC connects NPCs, factions, clues, secrets, locations and consequences to preserve narrative memory session after session.
learningGoals: Use entities as a living map of NPCs, locations, factions, clues, secrets and clocks.
Prepare sessions by connecting goals, scenes, available clues and secrets at risk.
Separate public truths, rumors and DM secrets so the player portal can be useful without spoiling the campaign.
Record decisions and consequences so Valdris reacts after every session.
Run a non-linear investigation where multiple clues can reveal the same secret.
includedMaterial: Eight prepared sessions with goals, scenes and table checklists.
Connected factions, NPCs, locations, clues, secrets, decisions and consequences.
Narrative canvas for the Valdris conspiracy.
Pressure clocks for cult surveillance and Veradis’s escape.
Public facts and DM secrets ready to reveal during play.

quickStart.title: How to start The Oracle’s Shadow
steps: Read the summary and open the Valdris conspiracy canvas.
Run session 1 with three clear doors: Oracle audience, Raven Tavern or City Archive.
At the end of each session, mark which clues appeared and which secrets are now at risk.
Advance clocks if the group accuses publicly without proof or if Veradis learns the vault is threatened.
Use the final decision to choose between reform, riot, escape or uneasy compromise.

ent_tpl_oracle_campaign_note.title: How to use this campaign
ent_tpl_oracle_campaign_note.summary: Run Valdris as a living conspiracy: clues open secrets, factions react, and every revelation changes the city.
ent_tpl_oracle_campaign_note.content: Use the campaign dashboard as a DM control panel. Keep active quests and public facts visible, keep the fraud, vault and faction compromises hidden until the group earns proof.

ent_tpl_oracle_valdris.title: City of Valdris
ent_tpl_oracle_valdris.subtitle: Port city ruled by faith, trade and fear
ent_tpl_oracle_valdris.summary: A white-stone coastal city whose political stability depends on the public authority of the Oracle.
ent_tpl_oracle_valdris.content: Valdris is prosperous, tense and watchful. Its council, temples, port guilds and noble houses all depend on the Oracle’s predictions, even when those predictions ruin ordinary lives.

metadata.atmosphere: port intrigue, religious pressure, political suspicion

ent_tpl_oracle_quest_broken_prophecy.title: The Broken Prophecy
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_broken_prophecy.subtitle: Main investigation arc
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_broken_prophecy.summary: The prophecy that brought the adventurers to Valdris is a magical forgery, opening the door to a twenty-year conspiracy.
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_broken_prophecy.content: Begin with a document that should be sacred but feels wrong: mismatched style, imperfect handwriting and small details that do not match older temple records.

metadata.objective: prove whether the prophecy is divine, forged or politically engineered

ent_tpl_oracle_faction_cult.title: Cult of the Oracle
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_cult.subtitle: Public faith, hidden fraud
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_cult.summary: A strict religious order split between sincere initiates, brutal inquisitors and a corrupt inner circle.
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_cult.content: The cult controls public fear through prophecies, fees, sermons and inquisitorial intimidation. Its command structure knows the Oracle is manufactured.

ent_tpl_oracle_faction_council.title: City Council of Valdris
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_council.subtitle: Government divided between stability and reform
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_council.summary: The ruling council is split between reformers loyal to Lena and conservatives compromised by Vantis.
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_council.content: The Council wants order above all else. Some members believe the Oracle; others suspect corruption but fear the city will collapse if the truth comes out badly.

ent_tpl_oracle_faction_guild.title: Thieves’ Guild
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_guild.subtitle: Contraband, information and survival
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_guild.summary: A criminal network controlling smuggling, gambling and secrets around the docks.
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_guild.content: The Guild fears the cult’s expansion but will not help out of charity. Its proof against Vantis is also its insurance policy.

ent_tpl_oracle_faction_truth_temple.title: Temple of Truth
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_truth_temple.subtitle: Discredited faith guarding real history
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_truth_temple.summary: A poor, marginalized order that denounces manipulated prophecies and protects old theological records.
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_truth_temple.content: The temple lacks force, money and public credibility, but it preserves sources that can dismantle the Oracle’s theology.

ent_tpl_oracle_faction_merchants.title: Merchants’ Consortium
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_merchants.subtitle: Trade power behind polite doors
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_merchants.summary: A coalition of merchant families with deep influence over tariffs, council campaigns and port logistics.
ent_tpl_oracle_faction_merchants.content: The Consortium is pragmatic. It does not need the Oracle to be true; it needs trade to continue and information to arrive before public votes.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_veradis.title: Veradis the Oracle
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_veradis.subtitle: Revered public prophet, hidden impostor
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_veradis.summary: The feared spiritual authority of Valdris has falsified divine prophecies for more than twenty years.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_veradis.content: Publicly serene and untouchable. Privately he is Jack Corvo, an exiled illusionist who murdered the real seer and built a citywide lie.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_senra.title: Mage Senra
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_senra.subtitle: Operator of the false divine voice
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_senra.summary: The illusionist who maintains the Oracle’s projection from a hidden control chamber, trapped by threats against her family.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_senra.content: If Senra stops channeling the resonance crystal, the voice and image of the Oracle collapse. She may confess if protected.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_lyra.title: Captain Lyra Stonehaven
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_lyra.subtitle: Guard captain with secret notes
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_lyra.summary: An honest investigator who suspects the cult but fears acting without proof could trigger civil violence.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_lyra.content: Lyra has documented disappearances at the docks and pressure from the Council. She can become a crucial ally if the group brings evidence rather than noise.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_kael.title: Kael Nightblade
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_kael.subtitle: Guildmaster with the ledger
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_kael.summary: A pragmatic criminal leader who holds accounting proof against Vantis and the cult as his life insurance.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_kael.content: Kael will trade truth for leverage. He can open the financial line of the conspiracy, but only if the group accepts an uncomfortable bargain.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_vantis.title: Lord Vantis
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_vantis.subtitle: Noble financier of useful prophecies
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_vantis.summary: A wealthy aristocrat who funds the cult in exchange for prophecies that destroy commercial rivals.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_vantis.content: Vantis is not devout; he is transactional. Cornered with documents, he may confess, betray Brann or reveal Veradis’s escape route.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_sera.title: Sera Moonwhisper
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_sera.subtitle: Priestess of the Temple of Truth
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_sera.summary: A young priestess guarding ancient chronicles that undermine the Oracle’s religious authority.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_sera.content: Sera has little worldly power, but she can interpret texts no one else dares to preserve. She is a moral anchor, not an easy solution.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_mira.title: Mira the Archivist
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_mira.subtitle: Keeper of burned records
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_mira.summary: The elderly head archivist survived the old fire and protects fragments that can expose the purge of true seers.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_mira.content: Mira refuses easy trust. Earning her help should feel like gaining access to Valdris’s wounded memory.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_mors.title: Inquisitor Mors
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_mors.subtitle: Cult enforcer
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_mors.summary: The fanatical commander of the cult’s guardians, responsible for intimidation, sabotage and disappearances.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_mors.content: Mors is conviction weaponized. He watches Lyra, threatens witnesses and becomes the cult’s direct answer to player progress.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_brann.title: Councilor Brann
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_brann.subtitle: Conservative who chose silence
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_brann.summary: A conservative council leader who knows enough corruption to act, but has preferred stability and bribes.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_brann.content: Brann is not the architect of the fraud; he is the respectable face of cowardice. He can be broken by records or Vantis’s testimony.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_dorian.title: Dorian Vex
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_dorian.subtitle: Consortium diplomat and double agent
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_dorian.summary: A polished intermediary who sells information to the cult and the Guild depending on who pays more.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_dorian.content: Use Dorian to explain why the enemy anticipates the group. Let suspicion build before revealing his role.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_asha.title: Asha the Widow
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_asha.subtitle: Victim of a paid false prophecy
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_asha.summary: A desperate woman seeking news of her son, unaware he died months before her audience with the Oracle.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_asha.content: Asha makes the fraud personal. Her case turns abstract corruption into cruelty at the table.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_drez.title: Captain Drez
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_drez.subtitle: Smuggler with the escape ship
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_drez.summary: Captain of the Silver Gull, hired to keep a ship ready for Veradis if the fraud collapses.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_drez.content: Drez is a pressure valve for the finale. If the group delays, Veradis can reach him and escape Valdris by sea.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_fenwick.title: Abbot Fenwick
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_fenwick.subtitle: Survivor of the old purge
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_fenwick.summary: An old guardian of the forest sanctuary who remembers the faith before the impostor’s rise.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_fenwick.content: Fenwick connects the investigation to the first Veradis and the ruins below the old temple.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_chamber.title: Oracle Chamber
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_chamber.subtitle: Public sanctum and hidden illusion room
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_chamber.summary: The ceremonial hall where Veradis delivers prophecies through staged sound, incense and arcane resonance.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_chamber.content: The chamber is built for awe: concave mirrors, hidden amplifiers, layered incense and a resonant crystal concealed near the altar.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_archive.title: City Archive
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_archive.subtitle: Burned memory of Valdris
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_archive.summary: A dusty archive whose historical wing still bears scars from the fire that erased the old seers’ records.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_archive.content: The Archive is where public history and private fear collide. Mira controls access to what survived the fire.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_ruins.title: Ruins of the Old Temple
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_ruins.subtitle: Burned remains above the vault
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_ruins.summary: Charred remains of the former Temple of Truth, destroyed during the impostor’s rise.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_ruins.content: Beneath the cracked altar stones lies the sealed access to the vault of proof.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_vault.title: Underground Vault
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_vault.subtitle: Twenty years of false prophecies
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_vault.summary: A hidden chamber under the old ruins containing ledgers, names, payments and the full archive of the fraud.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_vault.content: The vault stores records of 847 falsified prophecies over twenty years. It is the strongest proof in the campaign.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_port.title: Port of Valdris
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_port.subtitle: Trade artery and crime scene
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_port.summary: The city’s economic heart, where cargo, rumors and marked bodies surface together.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_port.content: Use the port for disappearances, Guild contacts, smuggling clues and Veradis’s possible escape route.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_raven_tavern.title: Raven Tavern
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_raven_tavern.subtitle: Neutral ground of whispers
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_raven_tavern.summary: A rough dockside tavern where the Guild keeps peace and information changes hands.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_raven_tavern.content: Torben can give the first crack in the official story: the old fire was not an accident.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_vantis_mansion.title: Vantis Mansion
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_vantis_mansion.subtitle: Luxury built on tailored prophecies
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_vantis_mansion.summary: The heavily guarded noble estate where trade wealth and hidden arrangements converge.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_vantis_mansion.content: A good location for infiltration, financial proof, political pressure or a cornered confession from Vantis.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_guard_barracks.title: Guard Barracks
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_guard_barracks.subtitle: Lyra’s fragile base of law
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_guard_barracks.summary: The fortified guard headquarters where Lyra keeps her private notes and trusted officers.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_guard_barracks.content: The barracks are not fully safe; Council pressure and cult surveillance reach even here.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_truth_temple.title: Urban Temple of Truth
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_truth_temple.subtitle: Poor shrine with dangerous books
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_truth_temple.summary: A diminished temple whose doctrine and relics threaten the Oracle’s legitimacy.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_truth_temple.content: Sera keeps the Chronicles hidden here, usually in a place that looks too poor to be worth searching.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_broken_prophecy.title: Text of the Broken Prophecy
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_broken_prophecy.subtitle: First Crack In The Fraud
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_broken_prophecy.summary: A holy parchment with stylistic and handwriting discrepancies against older temple records.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_broken_prophecy.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ledger.title: Guild Parallel Ledger
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ledger.subtitle: Financial Proof
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ledger.summary: A stolen ledger recording bribes, investments and payments connecting Vantis to the cult.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ledger.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vault_evidence.title: Vault Documentary Evidence
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vault_evidence.subtitle: Proof Of The Full Fraud
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vault_evidence.summary: The master archive of false prophecies stored in iron chests under the old ruins.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vault_evidence.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_illusion_components.title: Illusion Components in the Temple
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_illusion_components.subtitle: Mechanism Clue
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_illusion_components.summary: Exotic crystals and reagents hidden behind the altar preparation chamber.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_illusion_components.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_resonance.title: Magical Resonance of the Prophetic Voice
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_resonance.subtitle: Arcane Proof
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_resonance.summary: Residual arcane samples proving the divine voice was projected and manufactured.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_resonance.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_fire_records.title: Fragmentary Fire Records
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_fire_records.subtitle: History Clue
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_fire_records.summary: Charred minutes showing coordinated disappearances during the old archive fire.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_fire_records.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_chronicles.title: Chronicles of the True Seer
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_chronicles.subtitle: Religious Proof
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_chronicles.summary: An ancient theological manuscript protected by Sera Moonwhisper.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_chronicles.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_senra_confession.title: Senra’s Written Confession
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_senra_confession.subtitle: Defection Clue
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_senra_confession.summary: A draft letter hidden under Senra’s mattress, asking Lyra for protection.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_senra_confession.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ritual_marks.title: Ritual Marks of the Inquisition
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ritual_marks.subtitle: Violence Clue
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ritual_marks.summary: Burned marks on bodies recovered from the water, linking dock murders to the cult.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_ritual_marks.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_escape_plan.title: Impostor’s Contingency Plan
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_escape_plan.subtitle: Escape Clue
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_escape_plan.summary: A sealed coded letter describing Veradis’s route to the Silver Gull.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_escape_plan.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_military_letter.title: Sealed Eastern Front Letter
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_military_letter.subtitle: Human Cost Clue
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_military_letter.summary: An official letter confirming Asha’s son died before the Oracle’s planned false prophecy.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_military_letter.content: Deliver this clue through scene play, not exposition. It should answer one question while opening a larger one.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_fraud.title: The Oracle is a fraud
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_fraud.summary: Veradis has falsified divine prophecies for twenty years.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_fraud.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_voice_illusion.title: The divine voice is arcane illusion
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_voice_illusion.summary: The voice heard by petitioners is a projection sustained by Senra and a resonance crystal.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_voice_illusion.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_vantis_funds.title: Lord Vantis funds the cult
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_vantis_funds.summary: Vantis pays the Oracle for prophecies that favor his monopolies and ruin rivals.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_vantis_funds.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_vault_location.title: The proof vault lies beneath the ruins
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_vault_location.summary: The old temple hides a sealed archive containing twenty years of records.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_vault_location.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_true_veradis.title: The first Veradis was real
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_true_veradis.summary: The true Veradis Thorn was a genuine seer murdered by the impostor Jack Corvo.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_true_veradis.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_archive_fire.title: The cult burned the archive
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_archive_fire.summary: The historical archive fire was deliberate, meant to erase records of real seers.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_archive_fire.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_senra_defects.title: Senra can defect
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_senra_defects.summary: Senra maintains the illusion out of fear, not loyalty, and can be rescued.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_senra_defects.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_guild_proof.title: The Guild has proof
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_guild_proof.summary: Kael keeps a duplicate ledger as insurance against Vantis and the cult.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_guild_proof.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_escape_ship.title: Veradis has an escape ship
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_escape_ship.summary: The Silver Gull will sail if Veradis sends the alarm signal.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_escape_ship.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_dorian_spy.title: Dorian Vex is a double agent
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_dorian_spy.summary: Dorian sells information to the cult and the Guild depending on who pays more.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_dorian_spy.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_brann_knew.title: Councilor Brann knew
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_brann_knew.summary: Brann knew enough of the corruption and chose silence for stability and profit.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_brann_knew.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_asha_son_dead.title: Asha’s son is dead
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_asha_son_dead.summary: The prophecy Asha seeks will be false; her son died months ago at the eastern front.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_asha_son_dead.content: Keep this hidden until the group connects enough clues or earns testimony from the right faction.

ent_tpl_oracle_clock_cult_watch.title: Cult Watch Clock
ent_tpl_oracle_clock_cult_watch.summary: Each tick means the cult understands more of the party’s investigation and reacts more directly.
ent_tpl_oracle_clock_cult_watch.content: 1: initiates watch. 2: Mors receives reports. 3: Dorian leaks a plan. 4: ambush. 5: Senra is isolated. 6: Veradis prepares escape.

ent_tpl_oracle_clock_escape.title: Veradis Escape Clock
ent_tpl_oracle_clock_escape.summary: Tracks how close the false Oracle is to reaching the Silver Gull and leaving Valdris.
ent_tpl_oracle_clock_escape.content: Advance when the vault is found, Vantis is cornered, Senra defects or Mors fails publicly.

ent_tpl_oracle_decision_public_truth.title: Reveal the fraud publicly or contain it?
ent_tpl_oracle_decision_public_truth.subtitle: Central campaign decision
ent_tpl_oracle_decision_public_truth.summary: The party can expose the Oracle before the city or build a controlled case through Lyra, Lena and selected allies.
ent_tpl_oracle_decision_public_truth.content: Fast exposure cleans the lie but can cause panic. Containment buys time but can look like another cover-up.

ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_panic.title: Public Panic in Valdris
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_panic.summary: If the truth appears without enough proof, factions accuse each other and the city locks down.
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_panic.content: Use this when players reveal the fraud too early, lose key evidence or let Dorian turn suspicion into spectacle.

ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_reform.title: Reformist Transition
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_reform.summary: With proof and allies, Lyra and Lena can expose the fraud while keeping Valdris from collapsing.
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_reform.content: The Oracle falls, the Council fractures, and the Temple of Truth regains a cautious voice in public life.

ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_chaos.title: Temple Riot
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_chaos.summary: A rushed revelation turns liberation into unrest; the cult collapses, but Valdris burns politically.
ent_tpl_oracle_consequence_chaos.content: Use this ending if proof is partial, witnesses are dead or Veradis frames the party before fleeing.

ent_tpl_oracle_handout_audience_notice.title: Notice of Public Audiences
ent_tpl_oracle_handout_audience_notice.subtitle: Player handout
ent_tpl_oracle_handout_audience_notice.summary: The Oracle grants public audiences on Tuesdays and Fridays for a mandatory ten-gold offering.
ent_tpl_oracle_handout_audience_notice.content: Suggested text: By grace of the Oracle, all faithful petitioners may present grief, doubt or debt before the divine voice on the second and fifth day. The offering is ten gold crowns.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_public_audience.title: Public Audience of the Oracle
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_public_audience.summary: Opening scene showing faith, payment and theatrical prophecy.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_public_audience.content: Prepare the scene with a goal, one pressure point, one clue and one possible consequence.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_port_body.title: Body in the Harbor
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_port_body.summary: A marked corpse links dock disappearances to cult violence.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_port_body.content: Prepare the scene with a goal, one pressure point, one clue and one possible consequence.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_archive_mira.title: Mira’s Burned Archive
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_archive_mira.summary: A cautious archive scene where trust unlocks history.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_archive_mira.content: Prepare the scene with a goal, one pressure point, one clue and one possible consequence.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_guild_bargain.title: Bargain below the Docks
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_guild_bargain.summary: The Guild offers proof, but never for free.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_guild_bargain.content: Prepare the scene with a goal, one pressure point, one clue and one possible consequence.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_oracle_infiltration.title: Behind the Divine Voice
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_oracle_infiltration.summary: Infiltration reveals components, resonance and Senra’s role.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_oracle_infiltration.content: Prepare the scene with a goal, one pressure point, one clue and one possible consequence.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_vault_descent.title: Descent into the Proof Vault
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_vault_descent.summary: The party finds the master archive of the fraud.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_vault_descent.content: Prepare the scene with a goal, one pressure point, one clue and one possible consequence.

ent_tpl_oracle_scene_final_confrontation.title: The Oracle Falls
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_final_confrontation.summary: Final confrontation: expose, contain, arrest or let Veradis escape.
ent_tpl_oracle_scene_final_confrontation.content: Prepare the scene with a goal, one pressure point, one clue and one possible consequence.

ent_tpl_oracle_pc_elowyn.title: Elowyn Darkwater
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_elowyn.subtitle: Elven rogue — infiltration and deception
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_elowyn.summary: A skilled elven rogue specialising in infiltration and deception. She came to Valdris looking for something taken from her.
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_elowyn.content: Elowyn doesn't talk about what was taken from her, only that she's close enough to Valdris now that leaving without it isn't an option. She reads a room for exits before she reads it for allies.

ent_tpl_oracle_pc_camus.title: Brother Camus
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_camus.subtitle: Human cleric of the Temple of Truth
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_camus.summary: A human cleric consecrated to the Temple of Truth, seeking redemption for a past he would rather forget.
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_camus.content: Camus wears his faith carefully, like a bandage over something that hasn't fully healed. He'll forgive almost anyone else's mistakes faster than he'll forgive his own.

ent_tpl_oracle_pc_ragna.title: Ragna Ironsong
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_ragna.subtitle: Dwarven warrior — former city guard
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_ragna.summary: A dwarven warrior who served in the Valdris city guard for years and knows the city better than anyone.
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_ragna.content: Ragna spent a decade walking these streets in a guard's uniform and remembers which alleys the watch actually checks. She trusts procedure more than people, which makes the Oracle's unaccountability grate on her.

ent_tpl_oracle_pc_silas.title: Silas the Wanderer
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_silas.subtitle: Halfling bard — collector of stories
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_silas.summary: A halfling bard and collector of stories and secrets. Nobody quite knows where he came from.
ent_tpl_oracle_pc_silas.content: Silas collects stories the way other people collect debts — carefully, and always expecting to cash them in eventually. Half of what he says is true, and he's never in a hurry to say which half.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_noble_district.title: Noble Quarter
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_noble_district.subtitle: Walled residential district of Valdris's elite
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_noble_district.summary: The exclusive residential district of the Valdris elite, protected by high polished stone walls and private guards who bar the common folk.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_noble_district.content: Money built these walls to keep the city's problems out, and it mostly works. The people behind them like to believe Valdris runs itself — someone else's job to worry about the docks.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_guild_camp.title: Guild Hideout
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_guild_camp.subtitle: Secret Thieves' Guild base under the docks
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_guild_camp.summary: The Thieves' Guild's secret headquarters, hidden in the ancient sewer network beneath the docks. Its entrance is protected by traps and illusions.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_guild_camp.content: Kael runs the Guild's business from rooms nobody official ever mapped, protected by traps that only kill the careless. Getting an invitation here means someone already vouched for you.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_forest_shrine.title: Forest Shrine
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_forest_shrine.subtitle: Remote woodland temple of the Truth faith
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_forest_shrine.summary: A modest wooden temple hidden in the forest, several miles from Valdris's walls. It serves as a refuge for genuine believers in the Truth.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_forest_shrine.content: Out here, away from Valdris's politics and Vorn's theater, the faith the temple was built on still looks something like sincere. Abbot Fenwick keeps it that way on purpose.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_council_chamber.title: Council Chamber
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_council_chamber.subtitle: Grand parliamentary hall in the city hall
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_council_chamber.summary: The ornate parliamentary chamber in the city hall where councillors meet to debate Valdris's laws and taxes.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_council_chamber.content: Every law that touches Valdris gets argued here first, usually by people more interested in scoring points than solving problems. Aldric chairs it like a man used to winning before the debate starts.

ent_tpl_oracle_loc_docks.title: Harbor Docks
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_docks.subtitle: Rotting quays for smuggling and clandestine meetings
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_docks.summary: The rotting wooden quays of the harbor — ideal for smuggling arcane components and holding clandestine meetings late at night.
ent_tpl_oracle_loc_docks.content: Cargo moves through here that never appears on any manifest, and everyone with a stake in that arrangement prefers the dark. Lyra's official investigation and the Guild's unofficial one both start at the same rotting pier.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_aldric.title: Magister Aldric
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_aldric.subtitle: Supreme Magister, leader of the Valdris Council
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_aldric.summary: The supreme Magister and leader of the Valdris Council. A firm Oracle supporter who uses prophecies to legitimise unpopular political decisions, genuinely believing in their authenticity.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_aldric.content: Aldric isn't lying when he cites the Oracle — that's what makes him dangerous. He built two decades of policy on prophecies he genuinely believes, and he won't thank anyone who proves him wrong.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_lena.title: Councillor Lena Marsh
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_lena.subtitle: Leader of the Council's reform faction
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_lena.summary: Leader of the Council's reform faction. Long suspicious of the Oracle's secrecy and the commercial coincidences that benefit Lord Vantis.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_lena.content: Lena has been counting coincidences for years: which contracts follow which prophecies, who profits every time. She needs proof, not suspicion, before she'll move against a Magister.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_riku.title: Riku the Guard
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_riku.subtitle: Honest but frightened low-ranking guard
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_riku.summary: An honest but very frightened low-ranking guard. While patrolling the north docks he witnessed inquisitors dropping a suspicious bundle into the sea and now fears for his life.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_riku.content: Riku saw something he wasn't supposed to see and hasn't slept right since. He'll talk, but only somewhere he's sure nobody in a hood can hear him.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_torben.title: Torben the Innkeeper
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_torben.subtitle: Former smuggler, Raven Tavern owner
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_torben.summary: The burly and perceptive innkeeper of the Raven Tavern. A reformed smuggler who acts as a key informant for the Thieves' Guild in exchange for keeping his establishment free of trouble.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_torben.content: Torben got out of smuggling by trading favors for information instead of goods, and the Raven Tavern is where those favors get called in. He hears everything and repeats almost none of it — almost.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_cira.title: Cira the Shadow
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_cira.subtitle: Thieves' Guild field operative
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_cira.summary: An agile and lethal Thieves' Guild operative. She acts as the direct link between Kael and the port street gangs, and is the first to assess whether adventurers are trustworthy.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_cira.content: Cira decides in the first thirty seconds whether someone's worth Kael's time, and she's rarely wrong. Getting past her isn't about strength — it's about not looking like a liability.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_ola.title: Mistress Ola Brightstone
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_ola.subtitle: President of the Merchants' Consortium
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_ola.summary: The pragmatic president of the Merchants' Consortium. She cares nothing for the Oracle's theology or the Council's morality, as long as sea routes remain safe and free of excessive taxes.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_ola.content: Ola doesn't care if Veradis is real, false or somewhere in between — she cares whether the harbor taxes go up. That makes her easier to negotiate with than almost anyone else in this conspiracy.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_vorn.title: Herald Vorn
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_vorn.subtitle: Pompous official spokesman of the Oracle
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_vorn.summary: The pompous official spokesman of the Oracle. He manages public audiences, reads prophetic edicts, and decides — after a generous payment — who is worthy to receive Veradis's word.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_vorn.content: Vorn decides who gets to hear the Oracle speak, and that decision has a price. He believes his own performance a little more each year he gets away with it.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_initiate.title: Cult Initiate
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_initiate.subtitle: True believer, social obstacle
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_initiate.summary: Genuine novice believers in the cult. Truly convinced of Veradis's divinity, they handle maintenance, fake charity and minor surveillance around the temple grounds.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_initiate.content: The initiates believe every word, which is exactly why they're useful to whoever's really running things. Doubt would make them dangerous; faith makes them predictable.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_pell.title: Scribe Pell
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_pell.subtitle: Timid Council copyist with access to all correspondence
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_pell.summary: The timid Council copyist. His work lets him read all official correspondence between Magister Aldric, Lord Vantis and the Oracle's temple, recording incriminating minutes.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_pell.content: Pell copies every letter that crosses the Council's desk, and he's terrified of what he's already read. He wants to do the right thing — he just needs someone else to make it feel survivable.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_healer.title: Mistress Ilva
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_healer.subtitle: Port-district healer, informal witness
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_healer.summary: A healer from the port's low districts. She has clandestinely treated several sailors and guards with strange magical burns and unusual wounds caused by cult weapons.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_healer.content: Ilva treats wounds she's not supposed to recognize and asks fewer questions than she wants to. Her patients trust her more than they trust anyone with a title.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_pica.title: Pica the Fishmonger
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_pica.subtitle: Market gossip and social thermometer
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_pica.summary: A lively market trader who sells fish and spreads gossip. Her rumours are a chaotic mix of cult propaganda, palace chatter and distorted port truths.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_pica.content: Pica sells fish and rumors at the same stall, and you never know which one you're actually buying. Somewhere in her noise is a real thread, if the party's patient enough to sort it out.

ent_tpl_oracle_npc_bren.title: Sergeant Bren
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_bren.subtitle: Veteran guard, Lyra's trusted lieutenant
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_bren.summary: A veteran city guard sergeant. Cynical and battle-scarred, he is the only officer Captain Lyra fully trusts for secret investigations.
ent_tpl_oracle_npc_bren.content: Bren has seen enough of Valdris's politics to trust almost no one wearing a title, which is exactly why Lyra trusts him. He does the work quietly and doesn't ask who'll take credit.

ent_tpl_oracle_quest_silence.title: The Price of Silence
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_silence.subtitle: Side quest — cult intimidation at the port
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_silence.summary: Several port merchants and sailors are being intimidated by hooded cult figures to prevent them testifying about unusual cargo shipments.
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_silence.content: This should feel like static at first — an unrelated nuisance — until the party realizes the cult is protecting something specific at the docks, not just flexing muscle.

ent_tpl_oracle_quest_archivist.title: The Archivist and Her Secrets
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_archivist.subtitle: Side quest — earning the archivist's trust
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_archivist.summary: The elderly Mira holds historical documents of incalculable value but refuses to cooperate with the adventurers until they prove themselves worthy of her trust.
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_archivist.content: Mira isn't stalling for drama. She's been burned by people who used her archive and vanished, and she wants proof the party isn't the same.

ent_tpl_oracle_quest_port_blood.title: Blood at the Port
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_port_blood.subtitle: Side quest — magical murder investigation
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_port_blood.summary: Several bodies with mysterious magical burns have been found floating near the docks. Captain Lyra is running a parallel official investigation.
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_port_blood.content: Lyra's official investigation gives the party cover to ask questions nobody else can. Use that — let doors open because of her badge, not the party's charm.

ent_tpl_oracle_quest_traitor.title: The Inside Traitor
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_traitor.subtitle: DM-only — information leak investigation
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_traitor.summary: Evidence suggests the cult's inner circle anticipates the adventurers' secret plans. Someone is leaking confidential information.
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_traitor.content: This works best planted early and paid off late — a detail that felt like coincidence three sessions ago suddenly explains why the cult always seems one step ahead.

ent_tpl_oracle_quest_epilogue.title: Epilogue: Loose Ends
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_epilogue.subtitle: Narrative epilogue — power vacuum aftermath
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_epilogue.summary: The Oracle's collapse leaves a great power vacuum in Valdris. The alliances and decisions the adventurers made will determine the city's future.
ent_tpl_oracle_quest_epilogue.content: There's no single right answer to who should hold power in Valdris after this. Let the party's choices throughout the campaign — who they trusted, who they burned — write this ending for them.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_petitioner_fear.title: Fear Among the Faithful
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_petitioner_fear.summary: Several devout citizens who received adverse prophecies have stopped attending the temple, and their neighbours report they act terrified.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_petitioner_fear.content: Fear doesn't announce itself as evidence, but a pattern of empty pews and averted eyes adds up to something the party should notice before anyone tells them outright.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_merchant_payment.title: Suspicious Financial Transfers
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_merchant_payment.summary: Records of large anonymous donations transferred regularly to the temple treasury accounts.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_merchant_payment.content: Money leaves a trail even when people don't. These transfers are boring on purpose — nobody expects an auditor to be the one who brings down a prophet.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_torben_tip.title: Torben's Confidence
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_torben_tip.summary: Torben claims the fire that consumed the Temple of Truth and its archives twenty years ago did not start accidentally.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_torben_tip.content: Torben's not guessing. He was in the city twenty years ago, and he's spent two decades wondering if he should have said something sooner.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_port_bodies.title: Inquisition Ritual Marks
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_port_bodies.summary: Bodies recovered from the water bear an unmistakable brand burned into their shoulders — the cult's inquisitorial seal.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_port_bodies.content: The brand isn't subtle once you know what it means, but it takes someone who's seen it before to recognize it as more than a burn scar.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_lyra_notes.title: Captain Lyra's Secret Diary
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_lyra_notes.summary: Handwritten notes by Lyra detailing the loose ends the City Council ordered her to ignore.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_lyra_notes.content: These notes prove the Council didn't just fail to investigate — they ordered the investigation to stop. That's a different, more damning kind of complicity.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vault_entrance.title: Hidden Access in the Ruins
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vault_entrance.summary: An old topographic map revealing the temple's layout before the fire, including a sealed subterranean entrance.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vault_entrance.content: The map predates the fire, which means whoever drew it knew about a way in that the official story never mentions.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vantis_confession.title: Vantis's Testimony Under Pressure
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vantis_confession.summary: A declaration obtained from Lord Vantis after confronting him with his own account books.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_vantis_confession.content: Vantis doesn't confess out of guilt. He confesses because the numbers the party's holding are worse for him than the truth is.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_cult_disbands.title: Testimony of the Initiates
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_cult_disbands.summary: Voluntary statements from twelve temple novices after the inquisitors' arrest.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_cult_disbands.content: Not every believer was complicit, and these statements should make that distinction clear. Some of these novices are victims who need protecting, not villains who need punishing.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_eldertome.title: Tome of the Ancient Faith
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_eldertome.summary: An ancient Truth Temple record rescued from the ruins by Abbot Fenwick, containing the genealogy of genuine seers.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_eldertome.content: Fenwick kept this out of the fire on purpose, betting that someday someone would need proof that the old faith wasn't always a lie.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_final_truth.title: The True Veradis's Fate
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_final_truth.summary: An unmarked gravestone in the forest cemetery and associated notes found in the vault.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_final_truth.content: This grave answers the question the whole campaign has been circling: what actually happened to the real Veradis, twenty years ago.

ent_tpl_oracle_clue_forgery_tool.title: The Arcane Resonance Crystal
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_forgery_tool.summary: The powerful magical artefact used to generate and project the simulated voice of the Oracle.
ent_tpl_oracle_clue_forgery_tool.content: The crystal is the trick made physical — proof, the moment the party finds it, that every word Veradis ever spoke came from a device, not a god.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_lyra_suspects.title: The Captain Suspects but Fears to Act
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_lyra_suspects.summary: Captain Lyra has been documenting anomalies for months but lacks sufficient proof to move against the cult.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_lyra_suspects.content: Lyra's caution isn't cowardice. She's watched careers end for accusing the wrong people, and she needs the party to hand her something she can't be talked out of.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_kael_proof.title: The Guild Holds the Evidence
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_kael_proof.summary: The Thieves' Guild leader keeps incriminating accounting records as his personal insurance policy.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_kael_proof.content: Kael's insurance policy only protects him as long as it stays hidden. The party finding it changes his whole calculation about who's worth negotiating with.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_prophecy_count.title: Scale of the Fraud
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_prophecy_count.summary: The exact number of people who received false prophecies over twenty years — 847 documented cases in the vault.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_prophecy_count.content: 847 is a number too large to be an accident and too specific to be a rough estimate. Let that precision land before moving on.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_council_corrupt.title: The Conservative Councillor Knew
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_council_corrupt.summary: Councillor Brann discovered part of the corruption and chose not to act, accepting commercial bribes in exchange for silence.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_council_corrupt.content: Brann's silence wasn't ideological — it was transactional. That distinction should make him easier to expose than Aldric, and less sympathetic once exposed.

ent_tpl_oracle_secret_senra_exit.title: Vault Password
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_senra_exit.summary: The arcane passphrase to open the vault trapdoor, held secretly by Senra. Umbra Veritatis.
ent_tpl_oracle_secret_senra_exit.content: Senra didn't give this password up easily, and whoever has it now owes her something in return, whether they realize it yet or not.

rel_tpl_oracle_valdris_chamber.description: The Oracle Chamber dominates the monumental center of Valdris.

rel_tpl_oracle_valdris_archive.description: The City Archive sits near the Council buildings.

rel_tpl_oracle_valdris_port.description: The port is Valdris’s economic artery and most dangerous rumor market.

rel_tpl_oracle_valdris_tavern.description: The Raven Tavern operates in the lower dock districts.

rel_tpl_oracle_valdris_vantis.description: Vantis Mansion rises above the noble district.

rel_tpl_oracle_valdris_guard.description: The Guard Barracks anchor the city’s legal response to the crisis.

rel_tpl_oracle_valdris_truth_temple.description: The urban Temple of Truth survives in the old quarter.

rel_tpl_oracle_ruins_vault.description: The sealed vault lies under the burned altar stones of the old temple.

rel_tpl_oracle_cult_veradis.description: Veradis is the cult’s public object of worship and hidden architect.

rel_tpl_oracle_cult_senra.description: Senra works inside the cult under coercion, not loyalty.

rel_tpl_oracle_cult_mors.description: Mors commands the cult’s guardians and cleanup operations.

rel_tpl_oracle_council_lyra.description: Lyra’s guard answers to the Council even when she distrusts its orders.

rel_tpl_oracle_council_brann.description: Brann leads the conservative bloc that prefers silence to scandal.

rel_tpl_oracle_guild_kael.description: Kael commands the Guild and keeps its most dangerous proof.

rel_tpl_oracle_truth_sera.description: Sera guards the Temple’s most dangerous doctrine and manuscripts.

rel_tpl_oracle_truth_fenwick.description: Fenwick preserves memory of the faith before the impostor.

rel_tpl_oracle_merchants_dorian.description: Dorian represents the Consortium while selling information privately.

rel_tpl_oracle_cult_council.description: The cult shields Council decisions by dressing them as prophetic necessity.

rel_tpl_oracle_cult_truth_enemy.description: The cult publicly frames the Temple of Truth as heresy.

rel_tpl_oracle_cult_guild_enemy.description: The cult’s guardians threaten Guild smuggling and dock control.

rel_tpl_oracle_vantis_cult.description: Vantis’s monthly payments fund the cult’s security and tailored prophecies.

rel_tpl_oracle_mors_lyra.description: Mors spies on Lyra’s investigation and sabotages her evidence.

rel_tpl_oracle_dorian_party_plans.description: Dorian’s polite mediation hides the leak that lets the cult anticipate the party.

rel_tpl_oracle_drez_escape.description: Drez knows the ship, payment and departure signal for Veradis’s escape.

rel_tpl_oracle_broken_prophecy_fraud.description: The broken prophecy is the first verifiable crack in the Oracle’s authority.

rel_tpl_oracle_broken_prophecy_quest.description: The document gives the party a clear reason to investigate.

rel_tpl_oracle_ledger_vantis.description: The ledger confirms payments from Vantis to the cult.

rel_tpl_oracle_ledger_guild_proof.description: The ledger proves why Kael keeps leverage instead of speaking openly.

rel_tpl_oracle_vault_fraud.description: The vault records document the full twenty-year fraud.

rel_tpl_oracle_vault_vantis.description: The vault preserves payment records and beneficiaries.

rel_tpl_oracle_components_voice.description: The hidden components show how the divine voice is manufactured.

rel_tpl_oracle_resonance_voice.description: The magical resonance is incompatible with a natural or divine voice.

rel_tpl_oracle_fire_archive.description: The burned records show the old fire was coordinated, not accidental.

rel_tpl_oracle_fire_true_veradis.description: The fire records point to disappearances around the real seer’s death.

rel_tpl_oracle_chronicles_true_veradis.description: The Chronicles preserve the memory of a real Veradis before the impostor.

rel_tpl_oracle_senra_defect.description: Senra’s letter proves she can be turned if protected.

rel_tpl_oracle_senra_vault.description: Senra’s confession can include the word or route needed to open the vault.

rel_tpl_oracle_marks_cult.description: The ritual marks link dock violence to the cult’s inquisitors.

rel_tpl_oracle_marks_escape.description: The cult’s dock activity can lead toward Drez and the escape plan.

rel_tpl_oracle_escape_plan_ship.description: The coded plan describes Veradis’s route to the waiting ship.

rel_tpl_oracle_military_asha.description: The sealed military letter proves Asha’s prophecy would be a cruel lie.

rel_tpl_oracle_veradis_fraud.description: Veradis actively hides that he is Jack Corvo and has no prophetic gift.

rel_tpl_oracle_senra_voice.description: Senra knows exactly how the voice and projection are made.

rel_tpl_oracle_kael_ledger.description: Kael knows where the Guild ledger is hidden.

rel_tpl_oracle_mira_fire.description: Mira can reveal the surviving fire records if she trusts the party.

rel_tpl_oracle_sera_chronicles.description: Sera hides and protects the Chronicles of the True Seer.

rel_tpl_oracle_asha_letter.description: Asha’s upcoming audience points toward the military letter and the cruelty of the fraud.

rel_tpl_oracle_brann_knew.description: Brann hides that he chose profit and stability over truth.

rel_tpl_oracle_cult_clock_mors.description: As the cult watch clock advances, Mors becomes more direct.

rel_tpl_oracle_escape_clock_drez.description: As the escape clock advances, Drez prepares the Silver Gull.

rel_tpl_oracle_public_truth_panic.description: A public revelation without allies can trigger citywide panic.

rel_tpl_oracle_public_truth_reform.description: A public case supported by proof and allies can trigger a reformist transition.

rel_tpl_oracle_public_truth_chaos.description: An incomplete revelation can collapse into riot before justice arrives.

rel_tpl_oracle_scene_audience_chamber.description: The public audience scene happens in the Oracle Chamber.

rel_tpl_oracle_scene_port_marks.description: The harbor body scene can reveal the ritual marks.

rel_tpl_oracle_scene_archive_fire.description: The archive scene can reveal the burned records.

rel_tpl_oracle_scene_guild_ledger.description: The Guild bargain can put the ledger within reach.

rel_tpl_oracle_scene_infiltration_compon.description: The temple infiltration can reveal the illusion components.

rel_tpl_oracle_scene_vault_evidence.description: The vault descent delivers the campaign’s strongest proof.

rel_tpl_oracle_scene_final_decision.description: The finale forces a decision about how truth reaches Valdris.

fact_tpl_oracle_1.statement: Valdris’s public order depends on the Oracle’s authority, even when citizens fear the prophecies.

fact_tpl_oracle_2.statement: The prophecy that brought the adventurers to Valdris contains stylistic and magical inconsistencies.

fact_tpl_oracle_3.statement: The Oracle grants public audiences on Tuesdays and Fridays for a mandatory ten-gold offering.

fact_tpl_oracle_4.statement: Captain Lyra has investigated dock disappearances but has not published an official report.

fact_tpl_oracle_5.statement: The City Archive burned twenty years ago, destroying records of the old order.

fact_tpl_oracle_6.statement: Veradis has no prophetic gift; he is Jack Corvo, an impostor illusionist.

fact_tpl_oracle_7.statement: Senra sustains the false divine voice with the resonance crystal and can collapse the illusion by stopping her channeling.

fact_tpl_oracle_8.statement: Lord Vantis has funded the cult for years in exchange for profitable prophecies.

fact_tpl_oracle_9.statement: Kael’s ledger is one of the safest paths to proving Vantis’s payments.

fact_tpl_oracle_10.statement: The vault under the old temple contains records of 847 falsified prophecies across twenty years.

fact_tpl_oracle_11.statement: The true Veradis Thorn was a real seer murdered before the impostor’s rise.

fact_tpl_oracle_12.statement: The password that opens the vault is Umbra Veritatis.

fact_tpl_oracle_13.statement: Dorian Vex is the leak that can make the cult anticipate the adventurers’ plans.

fact_tpl_oracle_14.statement: Asha’s son died months before the Oracle’s planned answer to her paid audience.

fact_tpl_oracle_15.statement: If the party exposes the fraud without proof or allies, Valdris can fall into panic before reform begins.

fact_tpl_oracle_16.statement: Veradis has paid Captain Drez to keep the Silver Gull ready for escape.

fact_tpl_oracle_17.statement: [Theory]: Captain Lyra may be deliberately stalling the investigation to protect a relative implicated in the city guard.

fact_tpl_oracle_18.statement: Someone from the court or among the adventurers' usual intermediaries is systematically leaking their plans to Inquisitor Mors.

fact_tpl_oracle_19.statement: The scale of the Oracle's fraud spans hundreds of testimonies and the vault records could shake the foundations of the entire region.

fact_tpl_oracle_20.statement: Retcon: The rumour that the Oracle predicted the eastern plague was actually a lie manufactured and spread by the temple heralds.

fact_tpl_oracle_21.statement: Asha the widow has just handed over her last savings at the temple for a false prophecy about her son, unaware he died months ago.

fact_tpl_oracle_22.statement: Four fishmongers who reported unusual arcane cargo imports to the guard have disappeared under strange circumstances.

fact_tpl_oracle_23.statement: The deliberate archive fire destroyed the official seer genealogy, allowing the impostor to usurp Veradis Thorn without raising suspicion.

fact_tpl_oracle_24.statement: Councillor Lena Marsh is quietly seeking support among officers loyal to Captain Lyra to propose a Council audit vote.

fact_tpl_oracle_25.statement: The volume of the True Seer Chronicles held by Sera Moonwhisper is an authentic 300-year-old relic that exposes the cult's theological falsehoods.

prep.summary: Introduce the city, the Oracle’s social power and the first visible flaw in the prophecy.
prep.openingPrompt: Valdris rises above the coast in white stone and gray roofs. The temple bells ring before you see the gates, and every passerby seems to know when to lower their voice.
goals: Present Valdris as beautiful, prosperous and afraid.
Put the Broken Prophecy in the players’ hands.
End with a choice: audience, tavern, archive or port.
prep.notes: Keep clues flexible. The point is to preserve agency while making the investigation legible.

chk_oracle_s1_city.label: Show the Oracle’s influence in ordinary street behavior.

chk_oracle_s1_prophecy.label: Let the prophecy feel wrong before explaining why.

chk_oracle_s1_routes.label: Offer at least three investigation routes.

prep.summary: Turn suspicion into a criminal investigation through marked bodies, Lyra’s caution and port witnesses.
prep.openingPrompt: The tide brings in more than driftwood. A body knocks softly against the pier, shoulder burned with a mark no sailor wants to name.
goals: Introduce Lyra as useful but constrained.
Reveal that the cult uses violence, not only sermons.
Point toward Ilva, Riku, Torben or the Guild.
prep.notes: Keep clues flexible. The point is to preserve agency while making the investigation legible.

chk_oracle_s2_body.label: Describe the mark clearly enough to be recognized later.

chk_oracle_s2_lyra.label: Make Lyra ask for evidence, not blind trust.

chk_oracle_s2_pressure.label: Advance the cult watch clock if the party accuses the cult publicly.

prep.summary: Open the historical line: Mira, the old fire, and the first signs that the current Veradis is not the original one.
prep.openingPrompt: The archive smells of dry ink and stone dust. In the burned wing, black scars climb the wall like fingers trying to erase names.
goals: Earn Mira’s trust.
Recover fragmentary fire records.
Connect the fire to the old seers and the ruined temple.
prep.notes: Keep clues flexible. The point is to preserve agency while making the investigation legible.

chk_oracle_s3_mira.label: Give Mira a reason to resist before she helps.

chk_oracle_s3_records.label: Reveal one historical inconsistency, not the whole truth.

chk_oracle_s3_watch.label: Let the party notice they are being watched.

sess_tpl_oracle_04_guild.title: Session 4 — Bargain with the Thieves’ Guild

prep.summary: Make the financial conspiracy actionable through Kael, the ledger and a morally uncomfortable bargain.
prep.openingPrompt: Below the docks, water taps the stone in patient rhythm. Kael waits beside a locked iron coffer, smiling as if proof itself were a knife.
goals: Show why the Guild has leverage.
Let the party negotiate for the ledger.
Make Vantis visible as more than a rich suspect.
prep.notes: Keep clues flexible. The point is to preserve agency while making the investigation legible.

chk_oracle_s4_price.label: Set a price for Kael’s help that is useful but uncomfortable.

chk_oracle_s4_ledger.label: Let the ledger reveal Vantis without solving Veradis yet.

chk_oracle_s4_guild.label: Decide whether the Guild becomes ally, creditor or rival.

prep.summary: Reveal the mechanism of the false prophecy through infiltration, resonance and Senra’s possible defection.
prep.openingPrompt: Behind the sanctum wall, the divine voice is smaller: crystal hum, measured breathing, a woman whispering words meant to sound like heaven.
goals: Expose the illusion mechanism.
Present Senra as coerced, not simply guilty.
Give the party a way toward the vault password.
prep.notes: Keep clues flexible. The point is to preserve agency while making the investigation legible.

chk_oracle_s5_components.label: Make the illusion physical: crystals, mirrors, sound and residue.

chk_oracle_s5_senra.label: Let Senra ask for protection before confession.

chk_oracle_s5_mors.label: Use Mors if the party is too noisy or slow.

prep.summary: The cult anticipates the party. The investigation turns inward until Dorian’s role becomes actionable.
prep.openingPrompt: The trap is too precise. Someone knew not only where you would be, but which door you would choose when fear narrowed the room.
goals: Make the leak feel earned, not random.
Let suspicion fall on several plausible figures.
Reveal Dorian only if the party secures enough pattern evidence.
prep.notes: Keep clues flexible. The point is to preserve agency while making the investigation legible.

chk_oracle_s6_pattern.label: Show what the cult could only know through a leak.

chk_oracle_s6_false_leads.label: Let one innocent ally look suspicious for a scene.

chk_oracle_s6_dorian.label: Decide whether Dorian bargains, flees or frames someone else.

sess_tpl_oracle_07_vault.title: Session 7 — The Vault under the Ruins

prep.summary: Open the sealed vault and move from suspicion to undeniable proof.
prep.openingPrompt: The word Umbra Veritatis leaves the air colder. Under the ruined altar, stone unlocks like a memory deciding to speak.
goals: Use Fenwick or Senra to legitimize the route.
Reveal the scale of the fraud.
Force the party to decide what proof they carry and what they leave behind.
prep.notes: Keep clues flexible. The point is to preserve agency while making the investigation legible.

chk_oracle_s7_password.label: Require the password, a workaround or a cost.

chk_oracle_s7_scale.label: State the number of false prophecies clearly.

chk_oracle_s7_escape.label: Advance the escape clock when the vault is opened.

prep.summary: Resolve how Valdris receives the truth: public accusation, controlled legal case, riot, escape or reform.
prep.openingPrompt: The temple is full when the Oracle speaks. This time, every hidden mechanism, every witness and every lie is close enough to break.
goals: Let the party choose the form of revelation.
Give Veradis one credible escape attempt.
End with a visible change in Valdris.
prep.notes: Keep clues flexible. The point is to preserve agency while making the investigation legible.

chk_oracle_s8_truth.label: Ask exactly what proof is shown and to whom.

chk_oracle_s8_senra.label: Decide whether Senra breaks the illusion publicly.

chk_oracle_s8_ending.label: Choose reform, riot, escape or uneasy compromise based on play.

cvs_tpl_oracle_valdris_conspiracy.title: Valdris conspiracy map
cvs_tpl_oracle_valdris_conspiracy.description: Main canvas for running the Oracle fraud: public city, hidden mechanism, financial proof, old records and final decision.

note_tpl_oracle_table.title: Table advice
note_tpl_oracle_table.text: Run the mystery as a web, not a line: every clue should reveal one secret, point to one NPC and create one consequence risk.

cvs_tpl_oracle_characters.title: Factions and Characters
cvs_tpl_oracle_characters.description: Full cast canvas organized by faction. Shows every NPC and PC placed in their faction group, with key cross-faction connections highlighted.

cvs_tpl_oracle_investigation.title: Investigation Chain
cvs_tpl_oracle_investigation.description: Four-phase clue-to-secret investigation map. Phase notes guide the DM on pacing; edges show how each clue opens or confirms a DM secret.

cvs_tpl_oracle_locations.title: Valdris Location Map
cvs_tpl_oracle_locations.description: Location-centric canvas with each district as a group containing its NPCs, clues and access points — reduces false positives on empty-location alerts.
